description: Ilex ambigua is a species of flowering plant in the holly family known by the common names Carolina holly and sand holly. It is native to the southeastern and south-central United States, along the coastal plain from North Carolina to Texas, inland as far as Oklahoma, Arkansas, and Tennessee.
origin: TEN OO, NORTH CENTRAL USA, MIC, MSI OO, NCA OO

description: Ilex anomala, commonly known as Hawai'i holly, kāwaʻu, or ʻaiea in Hawaii, is a species of holly. It is native to the Hawaiian Islands and the Marquesas Islands and Society Islands of French Polynesia.
origin: SCI, PACIFIC, SCI OO, HAW, NORTH CENTRAL PACIFIC
synonyms: ["Byronia helleri", "Byronia sandwicensis", "Ilex hawaiensis", "Byronia hookeri", "Ilex sandwicensis", "Polystigma hookeri", "Ilex marquesensis", "Ilex taitensis", "Byronia anomala", "Byronia taitensis"]

description: Ilex aquifolium, the holly, common holly, English holly, European holly, or occasionally Christmas holly, is a species of flowering plant in the family Aquifoliaceae, native to western and southern Europe, northwest Africa, and southwest Asia. It is regarded as the type species of the genus Ilex, which by association is also called holly. It is an evergreen tree or shrub found, for example, in shady areas of forests of oak and in beech hedges.
origin: Western and Central Europe, northern Africa, western Asia
synonyms: ["Ilex aquifolium var. handsworthensis", "Ilex aquifolium var. argentea-purpurea", "Ilex balearica var. cordata", "Ilex aquifolium var. angustifolia", "Ilex aquifolium var. hybrida", "Ilex aquifolium var. longifolia-maculata", "Ilex aquifolium var. barcinonae", "Ilex aquifolium var. heterophyllum", "Ilex aquifolium f. arbutifolia", "Aquifolium lanceolatum"]

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day) | Climate: temperate
care.toxicity_note: Toxic to Dogs, Toxic to Cats, Toxic to Horses. Signs: Vomiting, diarrhea and depression. Leaves and berries are low toxicity.
common_problems: The English holly has no serious insect pests or diseases.
If the tree or shrub is grown in excessively wet conditions, it may develop root rot, anthracnose, tar spot, leaf blight or cankers.
common_pests: The English holly has no serious insect pests or diseases.
care.toxicity_symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea and depression. Leaves and berries are low toxicity.
care.toxicity_first_aid: Remove any remaining plant material from mouth. Rinse with water. Monitor for symptoms (vomiting, drooling, lethargy). Call poison control or vet if symptoms appear.
care.used_for: ["The wood is strong", "hard and dense and polishes well", "though it must be well dried and seasoned or else it warps badly. It is white", "except at the center of very old trees", "and is highly regarded by cabinet makers though it must be well seasoned."]
care.used_for_details: The wood is strong, hard and dense and polishes well, though it must be well dried and seasoned or else it warps badly. It is white, except at the center of very old trees, and is highly regarded by cabinet makers though it must be well seasoned.
care.edible_parts: Use caution. Human ingestion of berries can cause minor toxic reaction.

description: Ilex asprella, also known as rough-leaved holly and plum-leaved holly, is a deciduous shrub native in South East Asia. Ilex asprella is one of the few deciduous species in the family Aquifoliaceae.
origin: CHS JS, PHI OO, ASIA TROPICAL, CHS HN, CHS AH
AGE CN, BRAZIL, BZS, BZL, BZL ES
synonyms: ["Ilex caaguazuensis"]

description: Ilex canariensis, the small-leaved holly, is an endemic species of holly native to Macaronesian islands. It is a species of plant in the family Aquifoliaceae. It is found in the Macaronesian islands of Madeira (Portugal) and Canary Islands (Spain).
origin: MDR, AFRICA, MDR OO, CNY, MACARONESIA

description: Ilex cassine is a holly native to the southeastern coast of North America that grows from Virginia south down the East Coast to Florida, then west along the Gulf Coast to the Colorado River in Texas, with subspecies growing southward on the coast of the Gulf of Mexico as far as Veracruz, Mexico, and in Cuba, Puerto Rico, and the Bahamas. It is commonly known as dahoon holly or cassena, the latter derived from the Timucua name for I. vomitoria.
origin: Southeastern United States, Mexico, Bahamas, Cuba, Puerto Rico

description: Ilex coriacea, sometimes known as large gallberry or sweet gallberry, is a shrub in the holly family native to coastal areas in the United States from Virginia to Texas. It exists primarily as an understory plant in pine forests, and is sometimes managed by controlled burnings, resprouting from rhizomes.

description: The fruit is a black, globose, berrylike drupe on pollinated female plants. They ripen in early fall and will persist into spring. They measure ¼ to ⅓ inch in diameter. The fruits are hidden below the foliage. pedicels less than ¼ inch long.

description: Ilex glabra, also known as Appalachian tea, evergreen winterberry, Canadian winterberry, gallberry, inkberry, dye-leaves and houx galbre, is a species of evergreen holly native to the coastal plain of eastern North America, from coastal Nova Scotia to Florida and west to Louisiana where it is most commonly found in sandy woods and peripheries of swamps and bogs. Ilex glabra is often found in landscapes of the middle and lower East Coast of the United States. It typically matures to 5–8 ft (1.

description: Ilex integra, the elegance female holly, also called mochi tree, is an ornamental tree of the holly genus, which is native to parts of Asia, including Korea; Taiwan; the mid-southern regions of China; and Honshu, Shikoku and Kyushu in Japan. Its flower is light yellow. The species was botanically described in 1784.
origin: NNS, CHS MA, CHS HK, JAP, CHINA

description: Ilex laevigata (Pursh) A. Gray, commonly referred to as smooth winterberry, is a plant species in the Aquifoliaceae. It is native to the eastern United States, ranging from coastal areas of Maine to Georgia.

origin: JAP KY, ASIA TEMPERATE, JAP HK, JAP SH, JAP
synonyms: ["Ilex integra var. leucoclada", "Ilex leucoclada f. angustifolia", "Ilex leucoclada var. angustifolia"]

description: Ilex macropoda is a species of flowering plant in the holly family Aquifoliaceae, native to southern China, Korea, and Japan. A deciduous tree typically 13 m (43 ft) tall, it is found in mixed forests, forest edges, thickets, and roadsides, usually from 500 to 1,900 m above sea level. Local people make a tea by boiling its leaves.

Ilex micrococca, the thin-leaf holly, is a widespread species of flowering plant in the family Aquifoliaceae. It is native to Tibet, southern China, Hainan, Taiwan, northern Myanmar, Peninsular Malaysia, Vietnam, and Japan. A deciduous tree reaching 20 m (66 ft), it is found in evergreen broadleaf mountain forests from 500 to 1,900 m in elevation.
origin: MYA, CHT OO, VIE OO, ASIA TROPICAL, JAP HN
synonyms: ["Ilex micrococca f. luteocarpa", "Ilex polyneura", "Ilex pseudogodajam", "Ilex micrococca f. tsangii", "Ilex polyneura var. glabra", "Ilex micrococca var. polyneura", "Ilex micrococca var. longifolia", "Ilex micrococca f. pilosa"]

description: Ilex mitis is a tall, dense, evergreen tree that is indigenous to Sub-Saharan Africa and Madagascar. It makes an excellent fast-growing hedge for gardens - growing tall, straight and dense.
origin: ETH OO, CPP, OFS, WEST CENTRAL TROPICAL AFRICA, MOZ
synonyms: ["Scleroxylum mite", "Sideroxylon mite"]

description: Ilex mucronata, the mountain holly or catberry, is a species of holly native to eastern North America, from Newfoundland west to Minnesota, and south to Maryland and West Virginia.

Bright red or orange berry-like drupes appear on female plants after proper pollination. Persist on tree from September through February. Both sexes must be present to produce fruit (the plants are dioecious).

description: Yerba mate or yerba maté, Ilex paraguariensis, is a plant species of the holly genus native to South America. It was named by the French botanist Augustin Saint-Hilaire. The leaves of the plant can be steeped in hot water to make a beverage known as mate. Brewed cold, it is used to make tereré. Both the plant and the beverage contain caffeine.
origin: HAW MI, BZL TR, BZC, BZE CE, AGE CH
synonyms: ["Ilex paraguariensis var. genuina"]

description: Ilex pedunculosa, called longstalk holly, is a species of flowering plant in the genus Ilex, native to central and southern China, Taiwan, and Japan. A lanky shrub or shrubby tree typically reaching 5 m (16 ft) in the garden, it is quite cold hardy. It gets its specific epithet and common name from its long peduncle. The red berries are relished by birds. It is deer resistant.
origin: China, Central   South Japan
synonyms: ["Ilex pedunculosa var. continentalis", "Ilex pedunculosa var. senjoensis", "Ilex pedunculosa f. aurantiaca", "Ilex senjoensis", "Ilex impressivena", "Ilex purpurea var. leveilleana", "Ilex pedunculosa f. longipedunculata", "Ilex pedunculosa f. genuina", "Ilex morii", "Ilex pedunculosa f. continentalis"]

description: Ilex perado, the Macaronesian holly, is a species of holly endemic to Macaronesia, distributed throughout the Azores, Madeira and Canary islands. It is an important component of the natural high-altitude Macaronesian rainforest, known as 'laurisilva', found mostly at 500 to 1,200 m altitude but it also appears in forest formations at lower elevation.
origin: MDR, AFRICA, AZO, MDR OO, AZO OO

description: Ilex pubescens, the downy holly or pubescent holly, is a species of flowering plant in the family Aquifoliaceae, native to southern China, Hainan, and Taiwan. A shrub or small tree, it is found in a variety of habitats from sea level to about 1,000 m (3,300 ft). It is widely used in traditional Chinese medicine for a variety of ailments.
origin: CHC CQ, CHS MA, CHH, CHS HK, TAI

description: Ilex rotunda, commonly called the Kurogane holly, is an evergreen tree in the holly family (Aquifoliaceae). It is native to east Asia, where it is found in China, Japan, Korea, Taiwan, and Vietnam. Its natural habitat is in evergreen broadleaf forests, often in sunny areas such forest edges or on mountain slopes.
origin: VIE OO, ASIA TROPICAL, CHS HN, JAP HN, CHS AH
synonyms: ["Synstima rotundifolia", "Ilex koshunensis", "Ilex unicanaliculata", "Ilex rotunda f. xanthocarpa", "Ilex rotunda var. sinensis", "Ilex sasakii", "Ilex rotunda var. microcarpa", "Ilex rotunda f. xanthocarpa", "Ilex microcarpa", "Ilex rotunda f. xanthocarpa"]

description: Ilex serrata is a species in the Ilex (holly) genus and the family Aquifoliaceae. It is an evergreen shrub or small tree up to about 4.5 m high. In Japan where it naturally occurs, it is a hardy species with ovate dark green leaves, pink flowers and red berries on the female plant.

origin: KUR OO, JAP HK, JAP HN, EASTERN ASIA, JAP

description: Ilex theezans, also I. theæzans and I. theazans, is a species of the genus Ilex in the family Aquifoliaceae, native to Brazil and typically found in its Atlantic Forest. It is sometimes used as an adulterant of mate and sometimes confused with the yerba mate. In Brazil, it is sometimes called orelha-de-mico; but is more often simply known as congonha ("holly") or caúna (along with I. dumosa.
origin: BZC MS, BZE AL, AGE CN, BRAZIL, BZC
synonyms: ["Ilex theezans var. typica"]

care.toxicity_note: Human: strong toxic. Animal: toxic. Parts: Aerial part. (Source: TPPT/Agroscope)
common_problems: Leaf spots and powdery mildew occasionally occur.
Neutral to alkaline soils may cause chlorosis (yellowing of leaves) and even death.
common_pests: The native holly leafminer, Japanese wax scale, and southern red mites are the most frequently reported pests of hollies in the southeastern United States.

description: Ilex vomitoria, commonly known as yaupon or yaupon holly, is a species of holly that is native to southeastern North America. The word yaupon was derived from the Catawban yą́pą, from yą- tree + pą leaf. Another common name, cassina, was borrowed from Timucua. The Latin name was given by early European observers who misunderstood Indigenous purification ceremonies—where vomiting was part of the ritual—and incorrectly assumed that the plant itself was emetic.
origin: USA to Mexico

care.light_guide: NC State: Deep shade (Less than 2 hours to no direct sunlight) | Climate: seasonally dry tropical
common_problems: Leaf spot, leaf rot, tar spot, and powdery mildew are potential disease problems.
common_pests: Holly leaf miner, spider mites, whitefly, and scale are occasional problems.
care.used_for: ["Native Americans used the leaves to make a ceremonial emetic drink which", "when consumed in large quantities", "caused the purging indicated by the specific epithet. The leaves contain caffeine."]
care.used_for_details: Native Americans used the leaves to make a ceremonial emetic drink which, when consumed in large quantities, caused the purging indicated by the specific epithet. The leaves contain caffeine.
care.edible_parts: The young leaves may be picked, browned and dried in an oven, steeped in hot water and used to make Yaupon tea. It contains caffeine.
